mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and remains elevated for 2+ consecutive months, the restrictive monetary stance compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as market participants price in sustained cost-of-capital headwinds.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with restrictive rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window during tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll growth above 150,000 jobs signals robust labor market momentum and consumer spending capacity, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings growth expectations and reduced recession risk.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of months with PAYEMS growth > 150k jobs, or correlation between PAYEMS growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.08 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A joint signal combining rising retail sales (RSAFS) and declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates demand rotation away from housing toward consumer goods. This compositional shift reduces construction-sector earnings but boosts consumer discretionary and retail equity valuations, producing net positive SPX returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST month-over-month decline > 2%
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 697,876 million and HOUST at 1,309 thousand as of 2023-01-01
regime_hint: demand reallocation from housing to consumption
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 52% of instances meeting both trigger conditions, or the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST_decline) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression across manufacturing and energy sectors. This supply contraction produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of months with INDPRO decline > 0.3%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 3.5% year-over-year while the unemployment rate remains below 4% creates a stagflationary squeeze that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain or raise rates, compressing equity multiples. This regime produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 301.648 and UNRATE at 3.6% as of 2023-02-01
regime_hint: stagflation or tight labor market with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of instances meeting both trigger conditions, or the interaction term (CPIAUCSL × UNRATE_tightness) shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerating quarter-over-quarter above 2.5% annualized signals robust economic expansion and rising corporate earnings. This demand-side strength produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as investors increase equity allocations in response to growth acceleration.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 20,187.495 billion chained 2017 dollars as of 2022-10-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in > 50% of quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.10 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals labor market deterioration and recession risk. This triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as equity investors reduce exposure in anticipation of earnings headwinds from weaker consumer demand.
regime_hint: labor market deterioration or early-cycle recession warning
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances with UNRATE rising > 0.3pp over 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with declining inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals a favorable supply-side expansion with disinflation, reducing cost pressures on corporate margins. This productivity-driven scenario produces positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as investors reprrice earnings upward with lower discount rates.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth > 0.2% AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth < 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 102.9525 and CPIAUCSL at 301.648 as of 2023-01-01
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 52% of instances meeting both trigger conditions, or the interaction term (INDPRO_growth × CPIAUCSL_disinflation) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
